Off Calendar Jan-19-2022 Continued To Apr-05-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2021 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Lyft, Inc.'s demurrers are sustained with leave to amend - if plaintiff can amend in good faith. Plaintiff's complaint is a check-box form. Lyft was named a Doe defendant 21 months into the litigation. A complaint must allege "facts sufficient to establish every element of each cause of action." (See Rakestraw v. California Physicians' Service (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 39, 43.) Plaintiff's complaint does not do so, and form pleadings are not immune from demurrer. (People ex rel. Dept. of Transportation v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 1480, 1484.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Oct-12-2021 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2021 Continued To Oct-27-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1 And Request For Admissions Set One And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JEFFREY P. BOYKIN, Esq. (Tel: 888-602-5554) for the plaintiff;
Atty. JOSHUA R. JACHIMOWICZ, Esq. of Jachimowicz Law Group (Tel: 408-246-5500) for defendants Louis M. Acuna and Veronica L. Acuna.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS RASIER, LLC and UBER TECHNOLOGIES's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory 17.1 And Request For Admissions Set One And Request For Sanctions.

The Court indicates that plaintiff's counsel did not properly contest the tentative ruling and inform the court and counsel for the moving parties of his intent to appear at today's hearing. The Court notes that counsel for the moving parties is not present.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling, as follows: Requests for Admissions: Granted. The motion was unopposed. The court finds that plaintiff failed to comply with CCP 2033.220. The court further finds that plaintiff failed to make a reasonable inquiry or accessed readily available information that would enable plaintiff to admit or deny the Request. See Cal. Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2021), Para. 8:1348. Verified responses in compliance with CCP 2033.210 -230 to be served no later than September 28, 2021. Form Interrogatory #17.1: Granted. The motion was unopposed. The court finds that plaintiff did not make a good faith effort in responding to the interrogatory. When responding to interrogatories, the Discovery Act requires a party to make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information before responding to the interrogatories. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th 1496. A party cannot plead ignorance to information, which can be obtained from sources under his control. Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771, 782 This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Superior Court (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 161 CA 3d 151, 167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552. See Weil and Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2018) Para. 8:1051-1060. This means that an attorney can't just pawn off the responses to the client or spend an hour and dictate the responses off the top of his head. See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 CA4th 390. Verified responses to Form Interrogatory #17.1 in compliance with CCP 2030.210-230 to be served no later than September 28, 2021. Request for sanction is granted against plaintiff and his attorney of record, Jeffery P. Boykin, jointly and severely, in the amount of $2850.00. Sanctions to be made payable to the Client Trust fund of Wood, Smith, Henning & Berman no later than September 28, 2021.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim to the Judge Pro Tem at klgallo@discoveryreferee.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
